title: LRRK2 microtubule sedimentation binding assay

steps: Polymerize tubulin at around 2.5 mg/mL for 00:30:00 at 37 °C . Add Taxol for stabilization and incubate for another 00:10:00 at 37 °C .
Remove free tubulin by ultracentrifugation. 108628 x g, 37 °C, 15 minutes through a 64 % volume, glycerol cushion .
Resuspend the resulting microtubule pellet in the LRRK2 binding buffer .
Determine microtubule concentration by running an SDS-PAGE with actin standards.

Incubate desired amount of LRRK2 RCKW protein ( 200 nanomolar (nM) in our experiments) at Room temperature for 00:10:00 with varied concentrations of microtubules in the LRRK2 binding buffer . Assay can be modified to work with other proteins. We used the same assay to monitor LRRK1 RCKW binding.
Pellet the microtubules by ultracentrifugation. 108628 x g, 25 °C, 15 minutes
Quantify the depletion of LRRK2 RCKW by taking the supernatant and boiling for 00:10:00 in SDS containing buffer for running a gel.

Samples were run on 4-12% polyacrylamide gels (NuPage, Invitrogen) and stained with SYPRO-Red Protein Gel Stain (ThermoFisher) for protein detection. Binding curves were fit in GraphPad Prism (9.2; GraphPad Software) with a nonlinear regression hyperbolic curve.
creator: Mariusz Matyszewski
